raw.objective: “to evaluate the use of six different sets of CBZ pharmacokinetic parameters obtained by different authors about the potential implementation in a clinical phamacokinetic program as prior information” regarding a Portuguese population
raw.observations: 4 out of 6 model definitions performed acceptably when trying to predict concentrations in their population, using 1 measurement. Using 2 measurements allowed 5 out of 6 model definitions to predict adequately. Their results show that it’s not always needed to build new models.
raw.validation: No / Yes (Bayesian predictions using literature models)

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to evaluate the use of six different sets of CBZ pharmacokinetic parameters obtained by different authors about the potential implementation in a clinical phamacokinetic program as prior information” regarding a Portuguese population

validation.raw: No / Yes (Bayesian predictions using literature models)

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
